title: Presidential Sequestration Order for Fiscal Year 2027
summary: This executive order implements automatic spending reductions for fiscal year 2027 pursuant to the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act. The order, effective October 1, 2026, reduces direct spending budgetary resources across federal programs according to procedures established in section 251A of the Act. Such sequestration measures are triggered when deficit reduction targets established by Congress are not met through appropriations and revenue measures, resulting in proportional cuts to mandatory and discretionary spending categories as specified by the statutory formula.

title: Presidential Proclamation on Pharmaceutical and Pharmaceutical Ingredient Imports
summary: A presidential proclamation adjusting imports of pharmaceuticals and pharmaceutical ingredients into the United States. The Secretary of Commerce conducted an investigation into the effects of pharmaceutical imports on U.S. national security under section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962 and transmitted findings to the President. The proclamation implements adjustments to pharmaceutical import policy based on this investigation.

title: Proclamation Adjusting Tariffs on Aluminum, Steel, and Copper Imports
summary: This proclamation amends and strengthens previous executive actions regarding import tariffs on aluminum and steel, and extends similar trade adjustment measures to copper imports into the United States. The action modifies Proclamation 9704 from March 8, 2018 concerning aluminum imports, Proclamation 9705 from March 8, 2018 concerning steel imports, and Proclamation 10962 from July 30, 2025, consolidating and reinforcing trade policy measures affecting these three commodities. The proclamation appears designed to maintain or increase protective tariff measures on these specific mineral imports.

title: Executive Order on Citizenship Verification Requirements for Federal Election Participation
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to implement procedures for verifying citizenship status of individuals registering to vote in federal elections. The order is issued under presidential authority derived from the Constitution and federal statutes including the Help America Vote Act of 2002 and the National Voter Registration Act of 1993. The order establishes mechanisms to coordinate with relevant federal agencies to ensure voter registration processes include citizenship verification measures in compliance with existing federal election law requirements.

title: Memorandum on Compensation for Transportation Security Administration Personnel During Government Funding Lapse
summary: This memorandum directs the Secretary of Homeland Security and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to ensure that Transportation Security Administration officers and employees receive compensation during a period when the Department of Homeland Security is operating without appropriated funds. The memorandum addresses operational challenges to the aviation security system that have resulted from the extended funding lapse and seeks to maintain continuity of transportation security operations.
